title: Chef table seasonal evening
summary: A hosted dinner format for guests who want a slower evening built around a tasting menu, paired drinks, and direct attention from the kitchen team.
body: This event is designed as a flexible HoReCa showcase that works for tastings, compact celebrations, and hosted private dinners. The service rhythm leaves enough time for conversation while keeping the menu structured and clear.

Guests receive a curated seasonal menu, a dedicated seating plan, and coordinated pacing between courses. The format can be adapted for small brand gatherings, birthdays, or hospitality presentations.
The team can discuss dietary notes, timing, and room layout before confirmation so the event page doubles as a useful starting point for real inquiries.
highlights: Dedicated service flow for private groups
Flexible setup for celebrations or brand hosting

title: Family brunch weekend
summary: A relaxed weekend brunch for families and friends with simple dishes, warm drinks, desserts, and comfortable seating.
body: This brunch format is made for guests who want an easy weekend visit without planning a formal celebration. The atmosphere stays relaxed, family-friendly, and practical for different ages.
The menu can include breakfast dishes, light mains, desserts, coffee, tea, and seasonal drinks. Seating can be arranged for couples, families, or small groups of friends.
The event works well as a repeatable horeca activity because it encourages daytime visits and gives regular guests a simple reason to return on weekends.

title: Spa relax evening
summary: A calm evening focused on sauna, hammam, pool time, quiet rest, and light refreshments for small groups.
body: This event is designed for guests who want to slow down after a busy week and spend a quiet evening in the relax zone. It can work for friends, couples, or small private groups.
The format may include sauna or hammam time, access to rest areas, light drinks, and a calm service rhythm. The exact setup can be adjusted to the available facilities.
Before booking, guests can confirm time slots, guest capacity, spa rules, and optional refreshments with the team.
highlights: Quiet wellness format for small groups
